blocks.content:
**What you'll learn:** A quick tour of what’s new and better in Grafana 13. It all runs on Play, so you don’t need your own stack to follow along.

Each stop jumps you to a real example, where you can open it, click around, and see how the feature behaves in an actual UI.
Grafana SQL expressions let you use SQL to query and transform panel query results on the server after those queries run, and before the panel renders.
**SQL Expressions Showcase** — Open this dashboard to try SQL expressions hands-on.
Add this SQL expression to filter values above 1.33
Click **Run query** to execute the SQL expression.
blocks.tooltip: Execute the expression and see filtered results in the panel.

blocks.content: Congratulations! You've written your first SQL expression with Grafana!

Graphviz lets you visualize relationships, flows, and architectures using the Graphviz DOT language with live metrics from any Grafana data source.

Define diagrams as code, bind metrics to visual properties, and let Graphviz layout engines handle the rest. Perfect for service topologies, business processes, infrastructure architectures, and organizational structures that update automatically with your data.
**Graphviz** — Open this dashboard to try the new **Graphviz** panel visualization.
## Starting point

blocks.reftarget: div[data-viz-panel-key='panel-1'] section[data-testid='data-testid Panel header Customer Purchasing Process (traffic, revenue)']
blocks.content: Observe the panel in this dashboard titled **Customer Purchasing Process (traffic, revenue)**.

Note that the panel contains a diagram of a business flow for a hypothetical company's e-commerce checkout process.

The audience for this diagram would probably be more business-focused than purely technical, and the specifics of which microservices, frontends, etc., would be represented in aggregate for our hypothetical use case.

Our diagram has all the right data in it ... but it's a little plain, it needs something more to bring the data to life.

Let's make it look like the **Customer Purchasing Process (traffic, revenue, SLAs)** panel instead.

## Objective

We will bring this diagram to life by binding our data to visual properties by:

- Adding a rule to an existing edge (arrow) override that will color-code edges, and
- Creating a new node (box) override rule with automatic data matching to color-code nodes

## Adding data-driven colors to edges

Let's add color coding to edges by updating an existing override rule.
Navigate to the edit view to edit the panel

blocks.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid Panel editor content'] div[data-testid='data-testid Options group Edge overrides']
blocks.content: In the panel options, scroll down to the **Edge overrides** section
Note that **Autodetect** matched all of the edges we're targeting
This will **automatically** execute the steps required to add color-coding to the edges.

steps.description: For the **Threshold set**, provide **Conversion rate (percentage)** as the input

steps.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid Edge overrides Edge override rules field property editor'] input:nth-match(5)
steps.description: For the **Color field**, provide **conversion_rate** as the input.

steps.tooltip: Observe that all of the edges are now colour coded based on **conversion_rate**!

blocks.content:
## Adding data-driven colors to nodes

Let's add color coding to nodes by creating a brand new override rule.

blocks.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid Panel editor content'] div[data-testid='data-testid Options group Node overrides']
blocks.content: In the panel options, scroll down to the **Node overrides** section
Note that there are no node overrides yet. Click on **+ Add node override rule** button to create a new one.

blocks.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid Node overrides Node override rules field property editor'] button:text('All')
blocks.content: Click the **All** button to the right of the **Select Nodes by ID** input
Note that the default **Autodetect** match mode found matches for all nodes.
This will automatically execute the steps required to add color-coding (stroke color) to the nodes' outline.

steps.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid Node overrides Node override rules field property editor'] input:nth-match(4)
section[data-testid='data-testid Panel header Customer Purchasing Process (traffic, revenue)'] div[data-testid='data-testid panel content']
steps.tooltip: Observe that all of the nodes now have an outline colour based on the health of their services.
Note that the abandonment nodes have a default colour. Hmmm, that's not quite right!

blocks.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid Node overrides Node override rules field property editor'] input:nth-match(1)
blocks.content: Scroll back up to the **Select Nodes by ID** input at the top of **Node override rule 1**.
Remove **abandon**, **abandon_checkout**, and **bounce** nodes.
Note that those nodes no longer have the override applied, since they have no service.
This will automatically execute the steps required to add color-coding (filled color) to the nodes' outline.

steps.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid Node overrides Node override rules field property editor'] input:nth-match(6)

steps.tooltip: We're done! Now we can see that cart and checkout abandonment cost our company a lot of money! Also, both of the services behind those experiences have been having trouble meeting their SLAs.

blocks.content:
Congratulations! You've tried out our new Graphviz panel visualization!

Now, we can see that cart and checkout abandonment cost our company a lot of money! Also, both of the services behind those experiences have been having trouble meeting their SLAs.

---

Check out [Graphviz panel](https://grafana.com/grafana/plugins/grafana-graphviz-panel/) documentation for more information!

Grafana has revamped the Gauge visualization!

The new gauge introduces a Circular style with built-in sparklines, gradients, and accessibility improvements, such as Center glow.
**Gauges** — Open this dashboard to try the new and improved **Gauge** visualization.
Switch to **Circle** style to see the new circular gauge.
blocks.tooltip: Circular gauges support sparklines, gradients, and center glow.

blocks.content: Toggle **Center glow** to add a glow effect.
blocks.tooltip: This accessibility feature makes the current value easier to read at a glance.

blocks.content: Set the **Segment count** to break the gauge into segments.

Congratulations! You've tried out the revamped Gauge visualization. To find out more what else we have changed, check out [Revamped gauge visualization]([https://grafana.com/whats-new/2026-01-19-revamped-gauge-visualization/).

Grafana has revamped the **Table** visualizations so dashboards look sharper and tell a clearer story.

The table visualization, which includes performance improvements, introduces new cell types: **Pill** and **Markdown + HTML** cells.
**Table** — Open this dashboard to explore the table visualization.
Follow along to try some of the new Table features

steps.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid Cell options Cell type field property editor'] input[role='combobox']
div[data-testid='data-testid Cell options Cell type field property editor'] input[role='combobox']
steps.description: Type **Pill** to change the cell type and hit enter

blocks.content: Congratulations! You've tried out the revamped Table visualization. To find out more about what else we have changed, check out [New table visualization]([https://grafana.com/whats-new/2025-09-05-new-table-visualization-is-generally-available/]).

Styling panels in Grafana used to require a lot of manual work. Editing each panel individually, or duplicating one just to reuse its look, and then swapping out the query. 

With the new copy-paste styles feature, you can copy the styling from one panel and apply it to another in just a couple of clicks.
**Copy-paste styles** — Open this dashboard to try out the new copy-paste styles feature and follow the guided step below!

blocks.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid dashboard controls'] button[data-testid='data-testid Edit dashboard button']
blocks.content: Click **Edit** to enter edit mode.
blocks.tooltip: You need to be in edit mode to access panel style options.

blocks.reftarget: div[data-viz-panel-key='panel-8'] section[data-testid='data-testid Panel header Gauge with sparkline and gradient']
blocks.content: Find the **Gauge with sparkline and gradient** panel — you'll copy styles from this one.
blocks.tooltip: This panel's visual styling will be copied to another panel.
blocks.scrollContainer: div[data-testid='data-testid DashboardEditPaneSplitter primary body'] div[data-testid='data-testid DashboardEditPaneSplitter body container']

blocks.content: Follow along to see how copy-paste styles work in action!

steps.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid header-container'] button[data-testid='data-testid Panel menu Gauge with sparkline and gradient']
steps.tooltip: Open the panel menu to access style options.
steps.scrollContainer: div[data-testid='data-testid DashboardEditPaneSplitter primary body'] div[data-testid='data-testid DashboardEditPaneSplitter body container']

steps.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid portal-container'] div[data-testid='data-testid Panel menu item Styles']
steps.tooltip: Hover to reveal the style actions.
Copy the visual styling from this panel.

steps.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid header-container'] button[data-testid='data-testid Panel menu Gauge with sparkline and center glow']
steps.tooltip: Open the menu on the target panel.

steps.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid portal-container'] div[data-testid='data-testid Panel menu item Styles']
steps.tooltip: Hover to reveal the style actions.
Apply the copied styles to this panel.

blocks.content: Congratulations! You've tried out the new copy-paste styles feature!

Visualization Suggestions have been updated to provide more relevant suggestions and are now the default method for selecting a panel visualization.

When you preview a visualization using the updated Suggestions feature, we give you a full-sized preview of the suggestion in the panel editor before you select the suggestion.
**London Transport TFL** — Open this dashboard, then follow the interactive steps below to try the new and improved visualization suggestions.
Click **Change** to open the visualization picker.
blocks.tooltip: The updated picker now shows full-size previews of each suggestion.

blocks.content: Pick **Bar gauge - LCD** and watch the panel update in real time.
blocks.tooltip: Each suggestion shows a live preview before you commit to it.

blocks.content: Congratulations! You've tried out the new dashboard visualization suggestions feature!
